briefing.headline: Rig up a Drop Shot — steady winter approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Drop Shot is a strong choice for winter with clear water and stable conditions.

Johnston: On deep Lake Ontario smallmouth at 33 feet, use a drop shot with a 3/8-ounce weight in all conditions. On calm days, cast it out and let it sit — don't move it. On rough, windy days, cast past the fish and drag it through them.

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

primaryApproach.targets: Focus on creek channel, grass, flat. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.targets: Focus on creek channel, grass, flat. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Wheeler: Cherokee smallmouth were feeding up, not down — a dropshot dropped in front of them wouldn't get bit. Had to keep the bait 2 ft above their heads for them to come up off the bottom and eat

Hackney: 'For winter deep-water jig fishing on lowland reservoirs with stumps and channel swings in 6-8 feet, I use 16-20 pound fluorocarbon. On hard structure like rock I go 14-16 pound. Only downsize to 12 in extremely deep clear water'

KVD: 'Winter bass move shallower than most realize — if you can reach them with crankbait, jerkbait, or slow-rolled spinnerbait in 10ft or less, power baits are first option. Fish avoid expansive flats unless warming trend pushes them to gravel po...

Johnston: In cold water when fishing shallower 2- to 10-foot depths, anchor outside of a sand patch, boulder, or weed edge and work the area hard for multiple hookups. These bass are moving super slow — you really have to work an area over hard wi...

briefing.depthStrategy: Stable winter conditions position bass at 15-35ft on main lake structure — bluffs, steep points, channel bends. Fish are lethargic and grouped tight. Vertical presentations directly on structure are essential.
adjustIf: If conditions suddenly shift (pressure drop, wind change), be ready to speed up your approach — transitional moments produce big bites
If water clarity decreases from wind or rain, upsize and switch to brighter colors
If you get followers without commits, add scent or downsize your hook/weight
